description: Phase 6 — Semantic Density: Fusion Test. Same domain content, same compulsion language — fused compound identity (A) vs. explicitly split two-anchor identity (B) vs. P_d baseline (C). Tests whether linguistic fusion of themes creates the density, or whether domain content alone carries the weight.
notes: Fusion Test. A and B contain identical thematic content: distributed systems expertise + thoroughness/compulsion. The variable is structural: A fuses both themes into one compound identity (the compulsion is an attribute of the distributed systems engineer); B splits them into two separate 'You are' anchors (domain expertise stated, then behavioral drive stated independently). If A > B: fusion form creates the gravity well depth. If A ≈ B: domain content alone carries the weight and fusion is incidental. C is the P_d floor anchor.
